headlines.title: What is Burnham's path to becoming Labour leader and PM?
headlines.description: How could MPs who want Sir Keir Starmer to be replaced force a leadership contest and who could stand?

headlines.title: Farage blames Makerfield defeat on anti-Starmer votes
headlines.description: The Reform UK leader says he is "disappointed" with his party's performance in the key by-election.

headlines.title: Scottish Conservatives win first Westminster by-election in more than 50 years
headlines.description: The Tories take Aberdeen South, while the SNP wins in Arbroath and Broughty Ferry.

headlines.title: Sir John Curtice: Burnham's win against Reform represents remarkable personal success
headlines.description: Andy Burnham not only retained the share of the vote Labour won in the seat in 2024 but increased it.

headlines.title: Boy, 3, was attacked by crocodile at zoo as man bailed
headlines.description: Police say the boy remains in a "critical but stable" condition in hospital.

headlines.title: US-Iran talks postponed as Vance pulls out of Switzerland trip
headlines.description: The talks were expected to focus on implementing the deal to end the war signed earlier this week.

headlines.title: Die-hard Scotland fans fly 22 hours in tiny plane to World Cup
headlines.description: David Smith and Fraser MacIntyre flew across the Atlantic, with stops in Iceland, Greenland and northern Canada.

headlines.title: Plans to end gazumping with binding agreements in house sales shake-up
headlines.description: Sales agreements will be legally binding sooner and making sellers provide more home information up front are part of the planned changes.

headlines.title: Amber extreme heat warning issued as 35C heatwave approaches
headlines.description: Temperatures are expected to peak in the mid-30s Celsius on Monday and Tuesday with impacts on health and a risk of disruption to travel.

headlines.title: Lebanon says Israeli strikes kill 18 as Israel says four soldiers killed by Hezbollah
headlines.description: It comes a day after the US and Iran signed a deal to end their conflict, including fighting in Lebanon.

headlines.title: 'I'd be put off if he asked to split it': Who should pay on a first date?
headlines.description: Some insist on splitting the bill, others say the asker should pay, while many still see a man paying as romantic.

headlines.title: Five tips to keep your kids cool this weekend
headlines.description: Temperatures are set to rise over the next few days, and children can be especially vulnerable - so read on for tips to protect them.

headlines.title: Tories pick themselves up off the floor with unexpected Scottish by-election win
headlines.description: The Conservatives secured a comfortable victory over the SNP in Aberdeen South after throwing everything at the seat.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: Where will Prince George be going to school?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: We only found out we had the same sperm donor dad when we were in our 20s
headlines.description: Natasha, Gemma and Helen say finding each other has been a "fairy tale".

headlines.title: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
headlines.description: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

headlines.title: The pressure on the Church of England to ditch its slavery reparations plan
headlines.description: Will the Church's commitments still be delivered, or do shifting political winds mean there is no longer the will to do so?

headlines.title: Harry Kane says singing Wonderwall with fans one of his favourite moments
headlines.description: Harry Kane says singing 'Wonderwall' with fans after England's win over Croatia on Wednesday was one of his "favourite ever moments" in a Three Lions shirt.

headlines.title: How the Tartan Army captured the heart of Boston
headlines.description: An estimated 50,000 Scotland fans have visited the city and won new friends in the period surrounding Scotland's two group stage matches.

headlines.title: A day of tears, scuffles & history as injury mars Canada's moment
headlines.description: Canada's first-ever win at a World Cup finals should have been cause for pure joy - but was overshadowed by a horrific injury to Ismael Kone, and the angry and emotional scenes that followed.

headlines.title: Meet Merlin the Duck: Mexico's World Cup 'ambassador'
headlines.description: The BBC’s Will Grant was amongst the crowds swarming to meet Merlin, a pet duck who went viral for waddling around Mexico City in the Mexican team's jersey.

headlines.title: Traitors star says he lost life savings to a scam
headlines.description: The former contestant of BBC show The Traitors is speaking out about falling victim to the scam.

headlines.title: Obama moved to tears by wife Michelle's speech
headlines.description: The couple were opening a site in Chicago, which the former president described as a "celebration of community".

headlines.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to stand trial for rape
headlines.description: French prosecutors confirm Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi will stand trial for rape, in accusations dating back to 2023.

headlines.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital
headlines.description: Markos's beloved hen was not responding to any treatments - so he did the only thing he could think of.

headlines.title: London Marathon to be two-day event in 2027
headlines.description: The London Marathon will be held across two days in a special one-off edition of the event in 2027, organisers have confirmed.

headlines.title: Warning over 'fragile' public finances as borrowing rises
headlines.description: The UK borrowed £23.3bn in May, according to official figures, up almost a third on the same month last year.

headlines.title: UK actress charged with importing meth worth almost £157m into Australia
headlines.description: Emaa Hussen, 34, faces life in prison for allegedly trying to smuggle 320kg of meth hidden in bags of charcoal.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Burnham Wins in Makerfield - So What Happens Now?
headlines.description: Burnham wins Makerfield by-election, paving way for leadership challenge against Starmer.

headlines.title: Jill Biden reveals how she really felt about Joe Biden’s health whilst he was president
headlines.description: And Trump wants to give America’s top legal job to his former personal lawyer

headlines.title: Two chancers bluff their way into weddings to meet women
headlines.description: Owen Wilson and Vince Vaughn star in this classic 2005 comedy

headlines.title: Coventry play champions Arsenal in Premier League opener
headlines.description: Newly promoted Coventry City will travel to champions Arsenal in the first match of the 2026-27 Premier League season.

headlines.title: Why Morocco game could be greatest of all for Scotland
headlines.description: Scotland will need their best display since Steve Clarke took over to achieve the result against Morocco that will secure knockout tournament football for the first time, writes Tom English.
